purpose: Provide one deterministic session-aware wishlist state boundary for all buyer-facing controls and pages.
success_outcome: Valid-current-customer wishlist state loads and mutates backend truth independently of cart merge, while guest/logout/session-expiry retain no favorite data or browser persistence.
anti_goals: Do not implement wishlist page/card markup.
Do not persist favorites or pending intent in browser storage.
Do not change backend API/auth behavior.

success_checks: Successful current-customer retrieval permits wishlist even during merge_blocked.
Per-product pending/error and backend truth adoption are deterministic.
Guest/current-customer 401/logout/session-expiry retain no in-memory or browser-stored wishlist data.
evidence_required: Current-customer capability, merge_blocked independence, and storage assertions.
Typecheck, lint, T3 human checkpoint, and recovery note.

stop_conditions: FT-004 cannot provide a truthful current-customer success/401 capability signal.
Wishlist would become coupled to cart-merge or checkout readiness.
Backend contract cannot return authoritative mutation state.
required_handoff: Report changed_files, commands_run, evidence, scope_compliance, and blockers_or_none.
Run /verify and /red-verify; record HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and ROLLBACK_RECOVERY_NOTE before T3 closure.
